title: Implement config system with global and per-repo YAML merge
description:
Implement config system with global and per-repo YAML merge. Scope includes:
- Config file: ~/.config/nexus/config.yml (global) + .nexus/config.yml (per-repo, merged at load)
- Environment variable overrides: CHROMA_API_KEY, VOYAGE_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, MXBAI_API_KEY, NX_PM_ARCHIVE_TTL, NX_ANSWER
- Config dataclass with strongly-typed fields (no YAML parsing in-code; see METHODOLOGY.md)
- CLI commands (in config_cmd.py): nx config show (print merged config as YAML), nx config set <key> <value> (write to global config)
- Config precedence: env vars > per-repo config.yml > global config.yml > defaults
- Tests: unit tests for merge logic, env override, and round-trip set/show

title: Phase 6: Hybrid search + cross-corpus reranking + answer mode
description: Hybrid search: merge semantic + ripgrep results with 0.7*vector + 0.3*frecency scoring. Cross-corpus reranking via Voyage rerank-2.5. Mixedbread fan-out (--mxbai). Agentic mode. Answer mode (-a) via Haiku with cite formatting. Output formatters.

title: Implement nx pm init, resume, and status commands
description:
WHAT: Implement PM scaffolding (init), session resumption (resume), and status display using T2 {repo}_pm namespace.
WHY: Foundation for PM infrastructure; init replaces project-management-setup agent writing .pm/ files.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx pm init creates 5 standard docs + phase-1/context.md in T2 under {repo}_pm
- AC2: --project optional; auto-detected via basename of git rev-parse --show-toplevel
- AC3: All init docs: ttl=permanent, tags=pm
- AC4: Templates embedded in binary (no external template files)
- AC5: nx pm resume outputs CONTINUATION.md content, capped at 2000 chars
- AC6: nx pm status shows current phase (MAX phase tag integer), last-updated agent, open blockers
- AC7: Missing BLOCKERS.md treated as zero blockers (not error)

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_pm_init_creates_all_docs: verify all 6 docs created in T2
- test_pm_init_auto_detect_project: git repo auto-detected
- test_pm_init_idempotent: init twice does not duplicate (upsert)
- test_pm_resume_output: resume prints CONTINUATION.md content
- test_pm_resume_cap: content capped at 2000 chars
- test_pm_status_phase: phase number computed from MAX phase tag
- test_pm_status_no_blockers: missing BLOCKERS.md shows "none"

title: Implement T3 CloudClient initialization with VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction
description:
Implement T3 CloudClient initialization with VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction. Scope:
- chromadb.CloudClient initialization with CHROMA_API_KEY
- VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction(model_name='voyage-code-3') for code__ collections
- VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction(model_name='voyage-4') for docs__ collections
- VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction(model_name='voyage-01') for knowledge__ collections (default)
- Collection naming constraint: MUST use '__' as separator (NOT '::'). Valid pattern: ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,63}[a-zA-Z0-9]$. Examples: code__myrepo, docs__corpus, knowledge__pm__repo.
- Sanitization: strip non-alphanum/underscore/hyphen chars, truncate to 59 chars + 4-char hash suffix if over limit
- Guarded expire query (3-condition): {"": [{"ttl_days": {"": 0}}, {"expires_at": {"": ""}}, {"expires_at": {"": current_iso_time}}]}
- VectorStore protocol implementation (constructor injection — not imported directly by indexing modules)
- Tests: mock CloudClient, verify collection name sanitization, verify expire guard

title: Implement SessionStart hook with T1 init and PM-aware context injection
description:
WHAT: Implement SessionStart hook behavior: generate UUID4 session ID, initialize T1 scratch, detect PM project via T2 query, inject CONTINUATION.md or memory summary.
WHY: Automated session initialization gives agents context immediately on session start.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: UUID4 session ID generated and written to ~/.config/nexus/current_session
- AC2: T1 EphemeralClient initialized for the session
- AC3: PM detection via T2 query: SELECT 1 FROM memory WHERE project='{repo}_pm' AND title='CONTINUATION.md'
- AC4: PM project detected: inject CONTINUATION.md content (capped at 2000 chars)
- AC5: Non-PM project: output recent memory summary (10 entries, 500 chars each)
- AC6: Output format matches spec: "Nexus ready. T1 scratch initialized (session: {session_id})."

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_session_start_generates_id: UUID4 written to current_session
- test_session_start_t1_init: EphemeralClient accessible after start
- test_session_start_pm_detected: PM project gets CONTINUATION.md content
- test_session_start_pm_cap: content capped at 2000 chars
- test_session_start_non_pm: non-PM project gets memory summary
- test_session_start_memory_summary_cap: 10 entries max, 500 chars each

title: Port Arcaneum PDF extraction pipeline (PyMuPDF4LLM + pdfplumber + OCR)
description:
WHAT: Port Arcaneum PDFExtractor logic: PyMuPDF4LLM for markdown extraction (primary), pdfplumber for complex tables (fallback), Tesseract/EasyOCR for scanned pages (fallback).
WHY: PDF extraction is the foundation of the document indexing pipeline; porting proven logic avoids reinvention.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: PyMuPDF4LLM extracts text as structured markdown (headings, lists, bold/italic)
- AC2: pdfplumber fallback triggers for complex table-heavy pages
- AC3: OCR fallback processes scanned/image-based pages
- AC4: Extraction method recorded per chunk (pymupdf4llm_markdown, pdfplumber, ocr)
- AC5: No raw PDF bytes stored; only extracted text flows to next stage
- AC6: Graceful handling of corrupt/unreadable PDFs with clear error messages

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_pymupdf_text_extraction: text PDF produces structured markdown
- test_pymupdf_heading_preservation: headings extracted with correct levels
- test_pdfplumber_table_extraction: table-heavy PDF triggers pdfplumber, tables preserved
- test_ocr_scanned_page: scanned PDF page extracted via OCR
- test_extraction_method_metadata: each extraction records its method
- test_corrupt_pdf_handling: corrupt PDF produces clear error, not crash

title: Implement SessionEnd hook with T1 flush and TTL expiry
description:
WHAT: Implement SessionEnd hook that flushes flagged T1 scratch entries to T2 and runs expiry on both T2 and T3.
WHY: Ensures session findings are preserved and stale data cleaned up automatically on session close.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: Flagged T1 entries flushed to T2 with their configured destination (project+title or default)
- AC2: --persist entries auto-flushed to their configured destination
- AC3: nx memory expire runs (T2 TTL cleanup)
- AC4: nx store expire runs (T3 TTL cleanup with permanent entry guard)
- AC5: T1 scratch cleared after flush
- AC6: Partial flush failure does not prevent remaining items or cleanup

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_session_end_flushes_flagged: flagged entries appear in T2 after SessionEnd
- test_session_end_flushes_persist: --persist entries flushed with correct destination
- test_session_end_memory_expire: T2 expired entries removed
- test_session_end_store_expire: T3 expired entries removed
- test_session_end_clears_t1: T1 scratch empty after session end
- test_session_end_partial_failure: one flush failure does not block others

title: Implement ripgrep line cache with 500MB soft cap and mmap reader
description:
WHAT: Build flat path:line:content line cache per repo for ripgrep-based full-text search, with 500MB soft cap and memory-mapped reading.
WHY: Hybrid search merges semantic results with ripgrep exact-match results; the line cache enables fast local full-text search.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: Line cache file at ~/.config/nexus/cache/{repo-name}.lines with format path:line:content per line
- AC2: Cache built during indexing from repo source files
- AC3: 500MB soft cap: low-frecency files omitted when exceeded, with logged warning
- AC4: Omitted files remain searchable via semantic search (just not ripgrep)
- AC5: Memory-mapped reading for fast ripgrep queries
- AC6: Cache rebuilt on re-index

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_cache_file_format: verify path:line:content format per line
- test_cache_build_from_repo: index small repo, verify cache contains all lines
- test_cache_500mb_cap: mock large repo exceeding cap, verify low-frecency files omitted
- test_cache_omission_warning: exceeding cap logs warning
- test_cache_mmap_read: read cache via mmap, verify content matches
- test_cache_rebuild: re-index triggers cache rebuild with updated content

title: Implement nx store expire with guarded query for permanent entries
description:
WHAT: Implement TTL expiry for T3 knowledge__ chunks, with a guard ensuring permanent entries (expires_at="") are never deleted.
WHY: Empty string sorts BEFORE any ISO timestamp lexicographically; an unguarded expires_at < now query would delete permanent entries.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx store expire removes chunks where expires_at < current_ISO AND ttl_days > 0
- AC2: Permanent entries (ttl_days=0, expires_at="") are NEVER deleted
- AC3: Uses ChromaDB where filter: {"$and": [{"ttl_days": {"$gt": 0}}, {"expires_at": {"$lt": current_iso}}]}
- AC4: Reports count of expired entries removed

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_expire_removes_expired: insert chunk with past expires_at, verify deleted
- test_expire_preserves_permanent: insert permanent chunk (ttl_days=0), verify survives
- test_expire_preserves_future: insert chunk with future expires_at, verify survives
- test_expire_guard_empty_string: verify empty string expires_at not treated as expired

title: Implement git frecency scoring from commit history
description:
WHAT: Compute per-file frecency scores from git log: sum(exp(-0.01 * days_since_commit)) for each file.
WHY: Frecency scores weight recently-active files higher in hybrid search, mirroring SeaGOAT scoring.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: git log parsed to extract per-file commit timestamps
- AC2: Frecency formula: sum(exp(-0.01 * days_passed)) computed correctly
- AC3: All files in repo scored, including those with no recent commits (low score)
- AC4: Scores stored as frecency_score metadata on code__ chunks
- AC5: Score is computed at index time (known staleness limitation documented)

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_frecency_recent_commit: file committed today has score close to 1.0
- test_frecency_old_commit: file committed 365 days ago has low score
- test_frecency_multiple_commits: file with 5 recent commits has higher score than 1 commit
- test_frecency_no_commits: new untracked file gets 0 score
- test_frecency_formula_accuracy: known input dates produce exact expected scores

title: Implement Mixedbread fan-out with graceful degradation
description:
WHAT: Implement --mxbai flag to fan out search queries to existing Mixedbread-indexed collections via the Mixedbread Python SDK.
WHY: Read-only fan-out preserves access to existing Mixedbread data during transition; zero new ingest spend.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: --mxbai queries stores listed in config mxbai.stores
- AC2: Mixedbread results converted to Nexus result objects for unified processing
- AC3: Results included in combined set before reranking
- AC4: Missing MXBAI_API_KEY prints warning and skips fan-out (no error)
- AC5: Missing mxbai.stores config prints warning and skips
- AC6: nx doctor verifies Mixedbread SDK auth when --mxbai configured

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_mxbai_queries_stores: --mxbai queries configured store IDs
- test_mxbai_result_conversion: Mixedbread results converted to Nexus format
- test_mxbai_missing_key: MXBAI_API_KEY unset prints warning, search continues
- test_mxbai_missing_config: no mxbai.stores prints warning, search continues
- test_mxbai_results_in_rerank: mxbai results included in reranking pool

title: Implement nx memory search, expire, and promote
description:
WHAT: Implement FTS5 keyword search, TTL expiry cleanup, and T2-to-T3 promotion for nx memory.
WHY: Completes the memory bank replacement with search, lifecycle management, and tier promotion.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx memory search "query" returns FTS5 ranked results across all projects
- AC2: nx memory search "query" --project X scopes search to one project
- AC3: FTS5 MATCH syntax works (AND/OR/NOT, phrase matching)
- AC4: nx memory expire removes entries where julianday(now) - julianday(timestamp) > ttl
- AC5: Permanent entries (ttl IS NULL) never expire
- AC6: nx memory promote <id> copies entry to T3 knowledge__ collection (placeholder until Phase 3)

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_search_keyword: insert docs with known content, search finds correct ones
- test_search_scoped: search with --project returns only that project's matches
- test_search_ranking: more relevant doc ranks higher
- test_expire_removes_old: insert with ttl=1, mock time advance, verify deleted
- test_expire_preserves_permanent: permanent entry survives expire
- test_promote_copies_to_t3: promote entry, verify it appears in T3 (mocked until Phase 3)

title: Implement markdown indexing with SHA256 incremental sync
description:
WHAT: Implement nx index md <path> with SHA256 content hashing for incremental sync and voyage-4 embeddings to T3 docs__ collections.
WHY: Markdown/notes indexing with skip-if-unchanged avoids redundant API calls.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx index md <path> indexes markdown files from path
- AC2: SHA256 content hash stored as content_hash metadata
- AC3: Re-indexing skips files whose SHA256 matches existing content_hash
- AC4: Changed files fully re-chunked and re-embedded
- AC5: Chunks embedded via voyage-4, upserted to docs__{corpus-name}
- AC6: YAML frontmatter metadata included on all chunks

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_md_index_creates_chunks: index markdown dir, verify chunks in T3
- test_md_incremental_unchanged: index twice, second time skips (no re-embedding)
- test_md_incremental_changed: modify file, re-index, verify new chunks
- test_md_content_hash: SHA256 matches expected value for known content
- test_md_frontmatter_in_metadata: YAML frontmatter fields on chunks

title: Implement nx scratch flag/unflag/promote for T2 flush
description:
WHAT: Implement flag/unflag (mark entries for SessionEnd flush to T2) and promote (immediate T2 copy) for scratch entries.
WHY: Allows agents to selectively persist session findings to T2 either at session end or immediately.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx scratch flag <id> marks entry for SessionEnd flush to default destination (scratch_sessions/{session_id}_{id})
- AC2: nx scratch flag <id> --project X --title Y sets explicit T2 destination
- AC3: nx scratch unflag <id> removes flush flag and clears destination
- AC4: nx scratch promote <id> --project X --title Y copies immediately to T2
- AC5: Promoted entries exist in both T1 (until session end) and T2

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_flag_default_destination: flag without args sets scratch_sessions destination
- test_flag_explicit_destination: flag with --project --title sets custom destination
- test_unflag_clears: flag then unflag, verify no flush metadata
- test_promote_copies_to_t2: promote entry, verify exists in T2 with correct key
- test_promote_preserves_t1: promoted entry still in T1 scratch

title: Implement T2 SQLite schema with WAL mode, FTS5 virtual table, and sync triggers
description:
Implement T2 SQLite schema with WAL mode, FTS5 virtual table, and sync triggers. Scope:
- Schema: memory table with columns (id, project, title, content, tags, ttl, session_id, agent, created_at, updated_at)
- WAL mode: PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL; PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000 (required for concurrent Claude Code session safety)
- FTS5 virtual table: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5(content, project, title, tags, content='memory', content_rowid='id')
- Triggers: keep FTS5 in sync on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
- Upsert: INSERT OR REPLACE with auto-update of updated_at
- TTL: integer days stored in ttl column (NULL = permanent)
- Index: (project, title) unique index for deterministic retrieval
- Tests: concurrent write test, FTS5 search test, TTL expiry test

title: Phase 1: Project scaffold + T2 SQLite schema + nx memory CRUD
description: Project scaffold with pyproject.toml, src/nexus/ package tree, protocols module, T2 SQLite schema (WAL + FTS5), and nx memory put/get/search/list/expire commands. Foundation for all subsequent phases.

title: Phase 6: Hybrid search, cross-corpus reranking, answer mode, and output formatters
description:
Advanced search: hybrid scoring, cross-corpus reranking via Voyage rerank-2.5, Mixedbread fan-out, agentic refinement, Haiku answer synthesis, and rich output formatting.

SCOPE:
- Hybrid search: merge semantic + ripgrep with 0.7*vector + 0.3*frecency scoring
- Cross-corpus retrieval (independent per corpus, different embedding models)
- Voyage rerank-2.5 for unified ranking; --no-rerank for round-robin
- Mixedbread fan-out (--mxbai, SDK integration, graceful degradation)
- Agentic mode (--agentic, Haiku-driven query refinement, max 3 iterations)
- Answer mode (-a, Haiku synthesis with <cite i="N"> formatting)
- Output formatters: bat/pygments syntax highlighting, vimgrep, json, files, color/no-color, -B/-A/-C context, -r reverse

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: Hybrid scoring correctly weights 0.7*vector + 0.3*frecency for code corpora
- AC2: --hybrid warns when no code corpus in scope; ignored for docs/knowledge
- AC3: Cross-corpus results reranked via rerank-2.5 produce unified ranking
- AC4: --mxbai gracefully degrades when MXBAI_API_KEY unset
- AC5: Agentic mode refines queries up to 3 iterations, deduplicates results
- AC6: Answer mode produces cited synthesis with <cite i="N"> format
- AC7: All output formats (default, vimgrep, json, files) produce correct output
- AC8: min_max_normalize computed over combined result set, not per-corpus

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_hybrid_scoring_weights: known vector+frecency → expected combined score
- test_hybrid_no_code_warning: --hybrid with docs-only corpus → warning printed
- test_cross_corpus_reranking: results from different models → unified ranking
- test_mxbai_missing_key: MXBAI_API_KEY unset → warning, results still returned
- test_agentic_refinement_loop: mock Haiku responses → query refined, results merged
- test_answer_mode_citations: Haiku synthesis → <cite i="N"> tags in output
- test_output_vimgrep_format: results → path:line:0:content format
- test_output_json_format: results → valid JSON with expected fields

Context: /Users/hal.hildebrand/git/nexus/spec.md (Search, Hybrid, Cross-corpus, Answer mode sections)

title: Implement hybrid search scoring with vector + frecency weighting
description:
WHAT: Merge semantic search results with ripgrep line cache results using 0.7*vector_norm + 0.3*frecency_norm scoring for code corpora.
WHY: Hybrid search combines semantic understanding with file activity patterns for better code search results.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: vector_norm = min_max_normalize(cosine_similarity, combined_result_window)
- AC2: frecency_norm = min_max_normalize(frecency_score, combined_result_window)
- AC3: Combined score = 0.7 * vector_norm + 0.3 * frecency_norm for code corpora
- AC4: Non-code corpora (docs__, knowledge__): score = 1.0 * vector_norm (frecency undefined)
- AC5: --hybrid with no code corpus in scope prints warning
- AC6: Ripgrep exact-match chunks: vector_norm set to 1.0 before weighted sum
- AC7: min_max_normalize computed over combined result set (not per-corpus)

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_hybrid_scoring_formula: known vector+frecency values produce expected combined score
- test_hybrid_code_corpus: code results use 0.7/0.3 weighting
- test_hybrid_docs_corpus: docs results use 1.0 vector_norm only
- test_hybrid_no_code_warning: --hybrid with docs-only prints warning
- test_hybrid_ripgrep_exact: exact match gets vector_norm=1.0
- test_min_max_normalize: known values produce expected [0,1] range
- test_normalize_combined_window: normalization uses combined set, not per-corpus

title: Implement nx pm expire, promote, and close commands
description:
WHAT: Implement TTL-based PM doc cleanup, T3 semantic promotion, and close (archive --status completed alias).
WHY: Completes PM lifecycle management with housekeeping and convenience commands.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx pm expire removes TTL-expired entries from {repo}_pm namespace
- AC2: nx pm promote pushes PM docs to T3 knowledge__pm__* for cross-project semantic queries
- AC3: Promoted docs tagged with original PM metadata plus knowledge store type
- AC4: nx pm close is alias for nx pm archive --status completed
- AC5: Promote uses voyage-4 embedding function (knowledge__ collection)

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_pm_expire_removes_old: decayed PM docs with expired TTL removed
- test_pm_expire_preserves_active: active (ttl=NULL) PM docs survive
- test_pm_promote_to_t3: PM doc appears in knowledge__pm__* collection after promote
- test_pm_close_calls_archive: close triggers archive with status=completed

title: Implement session ID management with UUID4 generation
description:
WHAT: Generate UUID4 session IDs and persist to ~/.config/nexus/current_session for cross-command access.
WHY: Session ID is required by T1 scratch (per-session filtering), hooks, and agent provenance tracking.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: UUID4 session ID generated on SessionStart (or first nx command in session)
- AC2: Written to ~/.config/nexus/current_session as plain text
- AC3: All nx subcommands read session ID from this file
- AC4: File is overwritten on new session start (not appended)
- AC5: Missing file triggers generation rather than error

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_session_id_format: generated ID matches UUID4 pattern
- test_session_id_persistence: write and read back from file
- test_session_id_shared: two commands in same session read same ID
- test_missing_file_generates: delete file, next command generates new one

title: Implement agentic search mode with Haiku-driven query refinement
description:
WHAT: Implement --agentic flag for multi-step query refinement (max 3 iterations) powered by Claude Haiku.
WHY: Agentic search autonomously improves query quality, finding more relevant results than a single query.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: Initial query retrieves top results
- AC2: Haiku evaluates results and responds with {"done": true} or {"query": "refined query"}
- AC3: Refined queries retrieve additional results, merged and deduplicated
- AC4: Max 3 iterations enforced
- AC5: Uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY via anthropic Python SDK
- AC6: Pairs with --answer for synthesis after retrieval

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_agentic_single_iteration: Haiku says done on first try, results returned
- test_agentic_refinement: Haiku refines query, second iteration finds more results
- test_agentic_max_iterations: 3 refinements then forced stop
- test_agentic_deduplication: same result from different queries appears once
- test_agentic_missing_key: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset produces clear error

title: Phase 2: T1 EphemeralClient + nx scratch + session ID
description: In-memory ChromaDB via EphemeralClient with DefaultEmbeddingFunction (local ONNX). nx scratch put/get/search/list/clear/flag/promote. Session ID generation (UUID4 to ~/.config/nexus/current_session). SessionStart/SessionEnd hook scaffolding.

title: Scaffold Python 3.12+ project with click CLI entry point
description:
Scaffold Python 3.12+ project with click CLI entry point. Scope includes:
- pyproject.toml with all dependencies (version-pinned): chromadb, voyageai, anthropic, llama-index-core, tree-sitter-language-pack, pymupdf4llm, pdfplumber, flask, waitress, click, import-linter, ruff, mypy, pytest, pytest-asyncio
- SPDX license identifier decision: repo is AGPL-3.0 (see LICENSE). Add '# SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later' header to all Python source files. Skip for config, test fixtures, and __init__.py stubs.
- src/nexus/ package skeleton with __init__.py, __main__.py
- .importlinter config enforcing the 7 module boundary rules from ARCHITECTURE.md
- CI quality gates: pytest, mypy --strict, ruff, lint-imports
- pip install -e . must succeed

title: Implement AST code chunking via CodeSplitter with line-based fallback
description:
WHAT: Chunk source files using llama-index CodeSplitter (tree-sitter-language-pack) with line-based fallback for unsupported extensions.
WHY: AST-aware chunking produces higher-quality code chunks that respect function/class boundaries.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: CodeSplitter produces ~150 line chunks at function/class boundaries for supported languages
- AC2: Supported languages via tree-sitter-language-pack: Python, JS/TS, Java, Go, Rust, C/C++, etc.
- AC3: Line-based fallback for unsupported extensions with 15% overlap
- AC4: No overlap at function/class boundaries (AST mode)
- AC5: Chunks carry metadata: ast_chunked (bool), has_functions, has_classes, has_imports, line_start, line_end
- AC6: Version pinning in pyproject.toml for llama-index-core + tree-sitter-language-pack

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_ast_chunking_python: Python file with multiple functions chunks at function boundaries
- test_ast_chunking_java: Java file chunks at class/method boundaries
- test_line_fallback_unknown_ext: .xyz file falls back to line-based chunking
- test_line_fallback_overlap: line-based chunks have 15% overlap
- test_chunk_metadata_ast: AST chunk has ast_chunked=True, correct line numbers
- test_chunk_metadata_line: fallback chunk has ast_chunked=False
- test_chunk_target_size: chunks target ~150 lines

title: Port Arcaneum SemanticMarkdownChunker with YAML frontmatter extraction
description:
WHAT: Port Arcaneum SemanticMarkdownChunker for heading-aware markdown splitting and YAML frontmatter metadata extraction.
WHY: Markdown notes and docs need structure-preserving chunking that respects heading hierarchy.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: Markdown split at heading boundaries preserving document structure
- AC2: YAML frontmatter extracted into metadata fields (title, author, date, tags, etc.)
- AC3: Chunks carry section_title metadata from nearest heading above
- AC4: Long sections further split at paragraph boundaries
- AC5: Code blocks kept intact (not split mid-block)

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_markdown_heading_split: markdown with H1/H2/H3 chunks at heading boundaries
- test_frontmatter_extraction: YAML frontmatter parsed into metadata dict
- test_section_title_metadata: each chunk has correct nearest heading
- test_long_section_split: 1000-line section under one heading still gets chunked
- test_code_block_preserved: fenced code block not split mid-block

title: Implement nx pm phase transitions, search, and blocker management
description:
WHAT: Implement phase retrieval (nx pm phase N), phase advancement (nx pm phase next), FTS5 search scoped to PM namespaces, and blocker management (block/unblock).
WHY: Core PM operations for tracking project progress, finding decisions, and managing blockers.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx pm phase 2 retrieves phase-2/context.md from T2
- AC2: nx pm phase next: reads current N from MAX(phase tag integer), creates phase-{N+1}/context.md, updates CONTINUATION.md
- AC3: nx pm search uses FTS5 scoped to *_pm namespaces via GLOB (not LIKE, since _ is a wildcard in LIKE)
- AC4: nx pm search --project X further scopes to {X}_pm
- AC5: nx pm block "text" appends bullet to BLOCKERS.md (creates if absent)
- AC6: nx pm unblock N removes blocker by line number

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_phase_retrieval: init then get phase 1, verify content
- test_phase_next_advances: phase next creates phase-2 doc, updates CONTINUATION.md

- test_pm_search_glob_scope: search finds *_pm entries, not *_active entries
- test_pm_search_project_scope: --project restricts to one project
- test_block_appends: block adds bullet to BLOCKERS.md
- test_block_creates_file: block on fresh project creates BLOCKERS.md
- test_unblock_removes: unblock by number removes correct line

title: Implement cross-corpus retrieval and Voyage rerank-2.5 unification
description:
WHAT: Query multiple corpora independently (different embedding models), combine results, and rerank via Voyage rerank-2.5 for unified ranking.
WHY: code__ (voyage-code-3) and docs__ (voyage-4) embeddings are incomparable; reranking produces a meaningful unified list.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: Each corpus queried independently with its own embedding function
- AC2: Per-corpus top-k: max(5, (max_results // num_corpora) * 2) results fetched
- AC3: Combined results reranked via voyageai.Client().rerank(model="rerank-2.5", top_k=max_results)
- AC4: --no-rerank skips reranking, interleaves results round-robin
- AC5: rerankerModel configurable (default: rerank-2.5, alternative: rerank-2.5-lite)

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_cross_corpus_independent_query: code and docs corpora queried separately
- test_per_corpus_topk_calculation: verify over-fetch formula
- test_reranking_produces_unified_list: combined results reranked by relevance
- test_no_rerank_round_robin: --no-rerank interleaves results
- test_reranker_model_configurable: config.embeddings.rerankerModel respected

title: Phase 1: Project foundation, config system, T2 SQLite schema, and nx memory CRUD

title: Nexus — Self-hosted semantic search and knowledge system
Phase 7: nx pm full lifecycle (init/archive/restore/reference)
description:
Project management infrastructure using T2 for active PM docs and T3 for archived syntheses.

SCOPE:
- nx pm init (scaffold PM docs in T2 under {repo}_pm namespace)
- nx pm resume (output CONTINUATION.md for session injection)
- nx pm status (current phase, last-agent, open blockers)
- nx pm phase N / phase next (retrieve/advance phases)
- nx pm block/unblock (blocker management in BLOCKERS.md)
- nx pm search (FTS5 scoped to *_pm namespaces, GLOB not LIKE)
- nx pm archive (two-phase: Haiku synthesis → T3, then T2 decay with NX_PM_ARCHIVE_TTL)
- nx pm close (alias for archive --status completed)
- nx pm restore (reverse T2 decay within window, handle partial decay)
- nx pm reference (semantic search across pm-archive chunks, dispatch rules)
- nx pm expire/promote

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx pm init creates 5 standard docs + phase-1/context.md in T2
- AC2: nx pm resume outputs CONTINUATION.md content (2000 char cap)
- AC3: nx pm status shows phase number, last-agent, blocker list
- AC4: nx pm phase next increments phase and creates new context doc
- AC5: nx pm archive synthesizes via Haiku then stores in T3, then decays T2
- AC6: Archive idempotency: re-archive within 5 min skips re-synthesis
- AC7: nx pm restore reverses decay; handles partial expiry with warning
- AC8: nx pm reference dispatches correctly (semantic query vs project name lookup)

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_pm_init_creates_docs: init creates all 5 standard docs + phase-1 context
- test_pm_resume_content: resume outputs CONTINUATION.md, capped at 2000 chars
- test_pm_status_display: verify phase, agent, blockers in output
- test_pm_phase_next: advance from phase 1 to 2, verify new doc created
- test_pm_archive_two_phase: mock Haiku then T3 write, then T2 decay
- test_pm_archive_idempotent: archive twice within 5 min, second skips synthesis
- test_pm_restore_reverses_decay: archive then restore, verify ttl=NULL and tags restored
- test_pm_restore_partial_expiry: some docs expired then warning + surviving docs restored
- test_pm_reference_dispatch: quoted string then semantic, bare word then project lookup
- test_pm_search_glob_scope: search hits *_pm namespaces only, not *_active

title: Implement nx doctor health check for all dependencies
description:
WHAT: Implement nx doctor that verifies all external dependencies: nx serve running, ChromaDB cloud reachable, Voyage API key valid, Anthropic API key valid, ripgrep on PATH, git available, Mixedbread SDK auth (conditional).
WHY: Single command to diagnose configuration and connectivity issues across all Nexus subsystems.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: Check nx serve running (PID file + process check)
- AC2: Check ChromaDB cloud reachable (CHROMA_API_KEY set + connection test)
- AC3: Check Voyage API key valid (VOYAGE_API_KEY set + test embedding call)
- AC4: Check Anthropic API key valid (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set)
- AC5: Check ripgrep on PATH (which rg)
- AC6: Check git available (which git)
- AC7: Check Mixedbread SDK auth (only when --mxbai has been configured; skip otherwise)
- AC8: Color-coded output: green for pass, yellow for warning, red for fail

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_doctor_all_healthy: all checks pass, all green
- test_doctor_missing_chroma_key: CHROMA_API_KEY unset, that check red
- test_doctor_missing_voyage_key: VOYAGE_API_KEY unset, that check red
- test_doctor_serve_not_running: no server, that check red
- test_doctor_ripgrep_missing: rg not on PATH, that check red
- test_doctor_mxbai_skipped: no mxbai config, Mixedbread check skipped

title: Implement answer mode with Haiku synthesis and citation formatting
description:

WHY: Answer mode provides the primary agent interface for Q&A over indexed content.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: -a flag triggers Haiku synthesis after search retrieval
- AC2: Haiku instructed to cite sources using <cite i="N"> for single source, <cite i="N-M"> for ranges
- AC3: Output shows synthesis text followed by numbered source list with match percentages
- AC4: --content shows matched text under each cited source
- AC5: Uses anthropic SDK with claude-3-5-haiku-20241022 model
- AC6: NX_ANSWER=1 env var equivalent to -a flag

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_answer_mode_synthesis: search with -a produces synthesis text
- test_answer_mode_citations: synthesis contains <cite i="N"> tags
- test_answer_mode_source_list: sources listed with paths and match percentages
- test_answer_mode_content_flag: --content shows text under each source

title: Phase 5: PDF and markdown indexing pipelines
description:
Document indexing via ported Arcaneum extraction/chunking logic with Voyage AI embeddings.

SCOPE:
- Port Arcaneum PDFExtractor (PyMuPDF4LLM → markdown, pdfplumber fallback, OCR)
- Port Arcaneum PDFChunker and SemanticMarkdownChunker
- PDF indexing: nx index pdf <path> → voyage-4 embeddings → T3 docs__ collections
- Markdown indexing: nx index md <path> → YAML frontmatter, voyage-4 → T3 docs__
- SHA256 content hashing for incremental sync
- docs__ metadata schema (source_path, section_title, page_number, extraction_method, etc.)
- No local copy of raw content; chunks + embeddings + metadata only in T3

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: PyMuPDF4LLM extracts PDF text as markdown with structure preserved
- AC2: pdfplumber fallback handles complex tables correctly
- AC3: OCR fallback processes scanned PDFs (Tesseract or EasyOCR)
- AC4: Semantic chunking preserves document structure (headings, sections)
- AC5: Markdown YAML frontmatter extracted into metadata fields
- AC6: SHA256 hashing enables skip-if-unchanged incremental sync
- AC7: docs__ collection metadata matches full schema from spec

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_pdf_extraction_pymupdf: extract text PDF, verify markdown output
- test_pdf_extraction_pdfplumber_fallback: complex table PDF triggers pdfplumber
- test_pdf_chunking_structure: multi-section PDF → chunks with correct section_title metadata
- test_markdown_frontmatter_extraction: YAML frontmatter → metadata fields
- test_markdown_semantic_chunking: heading-based splitting preserves context
- test_incremental_sync_sha256: index file, re-index unchanged file → no re-embedding
- test_docs_metadata_schema: verify all required fields present on chunks

title: Implement repo registry and HEAD polling for auto-reindex
description:
WHAT: Build repo registry (repos.json) with per-repo state tracking and HEAD polling for automatic re-indexing on commits.
WHY: Multi-repo management with automatic re-indexing is core to the persistent server architecture.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx index code <path> registers repo path in ~/.config/nexus/repos.json
- AC2: Each repo entry tracks: path, collection name (code__{repo-name}), last HEAD hash, last index time
- AC3: HEAD polling runs per-repo every 10s (configurable via server.headPollInterval)
- AC4: HEAD change triggers re-indexing of changed files
- AC5: nx serve status shows each repo's indexing state and estimated accuracy
- AC6: Accuracy display uses SeaGOAT sigmoid pattern during indexing

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_repo_register: register repo, verify repos.json entry
- test_repo_registry_persistence: register, restart, verify still registered
- test_head_polling_interval: configurable interval respected
- test_head_change_triggers_reindex: mock HEAD change, verify reindex initiated
- test_accuracy_display: partial indexing shows percentage estimate
- test_multiple_repos: register 3 repos, each tracked independently

title: Phase 3: T3 CloudClient, nx store, basic nx search, and collection management

Cloud-backed permanent knowledge storage via ChromaDB CloudClient with Voyage AI embeddings.

- AC3: nx store creates knowledge__ collection and upserts chunks with full metadata
- AC4: nx store expire only removes entries where expires_at < now AND ttl_days > 0 (guards permanent)
- AC5: nx search returns ranked results from specified corpus with correct output format
- AC6: --corpus prefix matching resolves code/docs/knowledge to correct collection sets
- AC7: nx collection list shows all T3 collections with document counts

title: Implement nx serve daemon with Flask/Waitress and PID lifecycle
description:
WHAT: Build the persistent Flask/Waitress server with daemonization via Popen, PID file management, and lifecycle commands.
WHY: The server manages multi-repo indexing, HEAD polling, and ripgrep cache; it must run persistently in the background.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx serve start daemonizes via subprocess.Popen(start_new_session=True)
- AC2: PID written to ~/.config/nexus/server.pid
- AC3: Stale PID detection via kill(pid, 0); stale file removed, start proceeds
- AC4: If already running, start is a no-op with message
- AC5: nx serve stop sends SIGTERM and removes PID file
- AC6: nx serve status shows uptime and indexed repo count
- AC7: nx serve logs tails ~/.config/nexus/serve.log
- AC8: Port configurable via server.port config (default from config)

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_serve_start_creates_pid: start writes PID file with valid PID
- test_serve_stop_removes_pid: stop sends SIGTERM and removes PID file
- test_serve_stale_pid: write stale PID, start detects and cleans up
- test_serve_already_running: start when running is a no-op
- test_serve_status_display: status shows uptime and repo count
- test_serve_configurable_port: config server.port changes listen port

title: Implement nx pm archive with Haiku synthesis and two-phase commit
description:
WHAT: Implement archive operation: synthesize all PM docs via Haiku into a structured summary, store in T3 knowledge__pm__{repo}, then decay T2 entries.
WHY: Archives distill signal from noise into one permanent, searchable chunk per project.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: Phase 1 (T3): Haiku reads PM docs (5 standard + most recent, 100 docs / 100K chars cap)
- AC2: Synthesis follows template: Key Decisions, Architecture Choices, Challenges, Outcome, Lessons Learned
- AC3: Target 400-800 tokens, hard cap 1200 tokens; split at section boundaries if exceeded (max 3 chunks)
- AC4: Stored in knowledge__pm__{repo} with metadata: store_type=pm-archive, project, status, archived_at, phase_count, ttl_days=0
- AC5: Phase 2 (T2): UPDATE ttl=NX_PM_ARCHIVE_TTL (default 90d), tags replace pm, with pm-archived,
- AC6: If Haiku or T3 write fails: abort, T2 untouched
- AC7: Idempotency: existing T3 chunk with title="Archive: {repo}" within 5 min skips re-synthesis
- AC8: --status defaults to completed; also accepts paused/cancelled

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_archive_synthesis_content: mock Haiku, verify synthesis follows template
- test_archive_t3_metadata: verify all pm-archive metadata fields correct
- test_archive_t2_decay: after archive, T2 entries have ttl=90 and pm-archived tag
- test_archive_haiku_failure: Haiku API error aborts, T2 untouched
- test_archive_t3_write_failure: T3 write error aborts, T2 untouched
- test_archive_idempotent: archive twice within 5 min, second skips synthesis
- test_archive_status_options: completed, paused, cancelled all accepted
- test_archive_doc_selection: 5 standard docs always included, then by recency

title: Implement nx memory get and list with filtering
description:
WHAT: Implement nx memory get (deterministic retrieval by project+title or ID) and nx memory list (filtered listing).
WHY: Primary read paths for the memory bank; agents need deterministic retrieval and project-scoped listing.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx memory get --project X --title Y returns content by (project, title) key
- AC2: nx memory get <id> returns content by integer ID
- AC3: nx memory list --project X shows entries sorted by timestamp DESC
- AC4: nx memory list --agent Y filters by agent name
- AC5: Output includes id, title, agent, timestamp for list; full content for get
- AC6: Missing entry returns clear error message (not stack trace)

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_get_by_project_title: put then get by project+title returns correct content
- test_get_by_id: put then get by ID returns correct content
- test_get_missing_entry: get nonexistent key returns clean error
- test_list_by_project: insert 3 entries in project, list returns all 3 sorted
- test_list_by_agent: insert entries from different agents, filter works
- test_list_empty_project: list for empty project returns empty with message

title: Phase 4: nx serve persistent server and code indexing pipeline
description:
Persistent Flask/Waitress server managing multi-repo code indexing with git frecency and AST chunking.

SCOPE:
- Flask/Waitress persistent server (daemonize, PID file, SIGTERM lifecycle)
- Repo registry (~/.config/nexus/repos.json) with per-repo state
- HEAD polling (10s default, configurable via server.headPollInterval)
- Git frecency scoring: sum(exp(-0.01 * days_passed)) per file
- AST code chunking via llama-index CodeSplitter + tree-sitter-language-pack
- Line-based fallback for unsupported extensions (15% overlap)
- voyage-code-3 embeddings → T3 code__ collections
- Ripgrep line cache (flat path:line:content file, 500MB soft cap, mmap)
- nx serve start/stop/status/logs
- nx index code <path>
- Accuracy display (SeaGOAT sigmoid pattern)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx serve start daemonizes and writes PID file; stop sends SIGTERM
- AC2: nx index code <path> registers repo and triggers initial indexing
- AC3: HEAD polling detects new commits and triggers re-indexing
- AC4: Frecency scores computed correctly from git log
- AC5: AST chunking produces ~150 line chunks at function/class boundaries
- AC6: Line-based fallback works for unknown file extensions with 15% overlap
- AC7: Ripgrep line cache built correctly, respects 500MB soft cap
- AC8: code__ collection metadata matches schema in spec

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_serve_daemonize: start writes PID, stop removes it, stale PID detected
- test_repo_registry_crud: add/remove repos from registry, verify JSON persistence
- test_head_polling_detects_change: mock git HEAD change, verify re-index triggered
- test_frecency_scoring: known commit history → expected frecency values
- test_ast_chunking_python: Python file → chunks at function boundaries
- test_line_fallback_chunking: unknown extension → ~150 line chunks with overlap
- test_ripgrep_cache_build: index repo, verify line cache file content format
- test_ripgrep_cache_cap: exceed 500MB, verify low-frecency files omitted with warning

Context: /Users/hal.hildebrand/git/nexus/spec.md (Server Architecture, Code Repositories sections)

title: Implement nx store for T3 knowledge persistence with TTL metadata
description:
WHAT: Implement nx store command that persists files or stdin to T3 knowledge__ collections with TTL, tags, and full metadata.
WHY: Replaces current agent pattern of manually writing to memory bank files for long-term persistence.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx store file.md --collection knowledge --tags "x,y" reads file and upserts chunks to T3
- AC2: stdin mode: echo "..." | nx store - --collection knowledge --title "Title"
- AC3: --ttl format: Nd, Nw, permanent (default: permanent for nx store)
- AC4: Chunks carry metadata: source_agent, session_id, title, category, tags, store_type, indexed_at, expires_at, ttl_days
- AC5: Collection auto-created as knowledge__{collection} if not exists
- AC6: Large content chunked appropriately for embedding

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_store_file: store a file, verify chunks in knowledge__ collection
- test_store_stdin: pipe content, verify stored with --title
- test_store_ttl_permanent: default TTL is permanent (ttl_days=0, expires_at="")
- test_store_ttl_days: --ttl 90d sets ttl_days=90 and expires_at correctly
- test_store_metadata_complete: verify all metadata fields present on stored chunks
- test_store_category_flag: --category "architecture" sets category metadata

title: Implement nx collection management commands
description:
WHAT: Implement nx collection list/info/delete/verify for T3 collection management.
WHY: Operators need visibility into collection state, document counts, and the ability to clean up.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx collection list shows all T3 collections with document counts
- AC2: nx collection info <name> shows size, embedding model, last indexed timestamp
- AC3: nx collection delete <name> requires --confirm flag (safety gate)
- AC4: nx collection verify <name> spot-checks embedding health (random sample query)

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_collection_list: create 2 collections, list shows both with counts
- test_collection_info: create collection with docs, info shows correct stats
- test_collection_delete_requires_confirm: delete without --confirm is rejected
- test_collection_delete_with_confirm: delete with --confirm removes collection
- test_collection_verify_healthy: verify returns healthy for valid collection

title: Nexus: Self-hosted semantic search, knowledge management, and PM infrastructure
description:
Nexus is a Python 3.12+ CLI tool that replaces expensive cloud ingest (Mixedbread) with locally-controlled indexing, while keeping ChromaDB cloud as the permanent knowledge store. It synthesizes the best of mgrep, SeaGOAT, and Arcaneum into a single integrated tool for Claude Code agents.

STORAGE TIERS:
- T1: In-memory ChromaDB (EphemeralClient + DefaultEmbeddingFunction) — session scratch
- T2: Local SQLite + FTS5 — memory bank replacement
- T3: ChromaDB CloudClient + Voyage AI embeddings — permanent knowledge store

SUBSYSTEMS:
- nx memory (T2 CRUD + FTS5 search)
- nx scratch (T1 session-scoped working state)
- nx store (T3 knowledge persistence)
- nx search (semantic, hybrid, cross-corpus, agentic, answer mode)
- nx serve (persistent Flask/Waitress server for multi-repo indexing)
- nx index code/pdf/md (indexing pipelines)
- nx pm (project management lifecycle)
- Claude Code plugin (SKILL.md + hooks + slash commands)

PHASING: 8 phases, ~39 feature beads
Phase 1: Foundation + T2 + nx memory
Phase 2: T1 + nx scratch + sessions
Phase 3: T3 + nx store + basic search
Phase 4: nx serve + code indexing
Phase 5: PDF/markdown indexing
Phase 6: Hybrid search + reranking
Phase 7: nx pm lifecycle
Phase 8: Claude Code plugin

Context: /Users/hal.hildebrand/git/nexus/spec.md

title: Implement nx memory put with upsert, TTL parsing, and auto-capture
description:
WHAT: Implement nx memory put command that writes/upserts content to T2 by (project, title) key with TTL and auto-captured metadata.
WHY: Primary write path for the memory bank replacement; all agent memory writes flow through this.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx memory put "content" --project X --title Y upserts by (project, title) key
- AC2: stdin mode: echo "text" | nx memory put - --project X --title Y
- AC3: TTL parsing: Nd (days), Nw (weeks), permanent/never (NULL)
- AC4: Default TTL is 30d when omitted
- AC5: session and agent auto-captured from environment (CLAUDE_SESSION via current_session file)
- AC6: timestamp auto-set to ISO 8601 write time
- AC7: --tags accepts comma-separated tags

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_put_creates_new_entry: put with new project+title creates row
- test_put_upserts_existing: put twice with same key updates content
- test_ttl_parsing_days: "30d" parses to 30
- test_ttl_parsing_weeks: "4w" parses to 28
- test_ttl_parsing_permanent: "permanent" and "never" parse to NULL
- test_ttl_default: omitted TTL defaults to 30
- test_stdin_mode: pipe content via stdin with - argument
- test_auto_capture_timestamp: timestamp is valid ISO 8601

title: Phase 7: nx pm full lifecycle + Claude Code plugin integration
description: PM lifecycle: init/resume/status/phase/search/archive/close/restore/reference. Archive synthesis via Haiku with T3 permanent storage. Restore within 90d decay window. nx install claude-code (SKILL.md + hooks). nx doctor health check. nx config show/set.

title: Phase 4: nx serve (Flask/Waitress) + code indexing
description: Persistent Flask/Waitress server with multi-repo registry. Code indexing: git frecency scoring (ported from SeaGOAT), AST chunking via llama-index CodeSplitter + tree-sitter-language-pack, voyage-code-3 embeddings, ripgrep mmap line cache. HEAD polling for auto-reindex.

title: Implement code embedding via voyage-code-3 and T3 code__ upsert
description:
WHAT: Embed code chunks using VoyageAIEmbeddingFunction(voyage-code-3) and upsert to T3 code__{repo-name} collections with full metadata schema.
WHY: Final step in the code indexing pipeline; connects chunking to permanent T3 storage.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: Chunks embedded via voyage-code-3 model
- AC2: Upserted to code__{repo-name} collection in T3
- AC3: Full metadata schema: file_path, filename, file_extension, programming_language, corpus, store_type, git_project_name, git_branch, git_commit_hash, git_remote_url, line_start, line_end, chunk_index, chunk_count, ast_chunked, has_functions, has_classes, has_imports, frecency_score, embedding_model, indexed_at, content_hash
- AC4: content_hash uses git object ID for staleness detection
- AC5: Incremental: skip files whose content_hash matches existing chunks

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_code_embedding_model: verify voyage-code-3 used (not default)
- test_code_upsert_collection_name: repo "myrepo" creates code__myrepo collection
- test_code_metadata_complete: all metadata fields present on upserted chunks
- test_code_incremental_skip: unchanged file (same content_hash) skipped on re-index
- test_code_content_hash_git: content_hash matches git object ID

title: Implement nx pm restore and reference commands
description:
WHAT: Implement restore (reverse T2 decay within window) and reference (semantic search across archived syntheses).
WHY: Restore provides a safety net for prematurely archived projects; reference enables institutional memory queries.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: nx pm restore reverses T2 decay: SET ttl=NULL, replace pm-archived with pm in tags
- AC2: Partial decay handled: surviving docs restored, expired docs listed in warning
- AC3: Full decay: error with suggestion to use nx pm reference or nx pm init
- AC4: T3 synthesis chunk NOT deleted on restore (remains as reference)
- AC5: nx pm reference dispatches: quoted string/spaces/? as semantic query, bare identifier as project name lookup
- AC6: Semantic query: nx search <query> --corpus knowledge --where store_type=pm-archive
- AC7: Project lookup: collection.get(where={"store_type": "pm-archive", "project": "<arg>"})

TEST CASES (TDD):
- test_restore_reverses_decay: archive then restore, verify ttl=NULL and pm tag restored
- test_restore_partial_expiry: some docs expired, surviving restored with warning
- test_restore_full_expiry: all expired, error with suggestions
- test_restore_preserves_t3: T3 synthesis chunk still present after restore
- test_reference_semantic_query: quoted string triggers semantic search
- test_reference_project_lookup: bare identifier triggers metadata-only lookup
- test_reference_no_arg_prompts: no argument prompts for query interactively

title: Phase 5: PDF/markdown indexing pipelines
description: PDF pipeline: port Arcaneum PDFExtractor (PyMuPDF4LLM + pdfplumber fallback + OCR), PDFChunker, voyage-4 embeddings to T3 docs__ collections. Markdown pipeline: port SemanticMarkdownChunker, YAML frontmatter extraction, SHA256 content hashing for incremental sync.
